description: Declare Workflow Runtime V2 Temporal runs as the only supported execution authority for new EE workflow runs during hard cutover.
Branch all relevant run-control APIs on `run.engine` so Temporal runs cannot accidentally flow through legacy DB-runtime execution paths.
Remove the hand-rolled Temporal workflow JSONata evaluator as a separate workflow-expression contract.
Adopt a single canonical workflow-expression contract for Temporal-native execution so publish validation and runtime evaluation share the same semantics.
Preserve exact allowed-function parity in Temporal expression evaluation, including `nowIso()`.
Preserve exact expression-source normalization behavior in Temporal evaluation so authored expressions parse the same way across validation and execution.
Preserve canonical disallowed-function rejection behavior for Temporal-native expression evaluation.
Preserve canonical JSON-serializability and max-output-size guardrails for Temporal-native expression results.
Ensure Temporal-native expression evaluation remains deterministic under replay while still honoring the canonical workflow-expression contract.
Block `resumeWorkflowRunAction` from invoking legacy DB-runtime resume behavior for Temporal runs.
Return an explicit unsupported-action error when resume is requested for a Temporal run without a Temporal-native resume contract.
Block `retryWorkflowRunAction` from invoking legacy DB-runtime retry behavior for Temporal runs.
Return an explicit unsupported-action error when retry is requested for a Temporal run without a Temporal-native retry contract.
Block any Temporal run path that still calls `WorkflowRuntimeV2.executeRun(...)` from operator or event-ingress control surfaces.
Make Temporal-run cancellation go through Temporal first instead of optimistically projecting `CANCELED` in the database.
Prevent cancel failures against Temporal from silently leaving the DB projection canceled while execution may still be active.
Add actionable operator-facing error messaging for unsupported Temporal run-control actions.
Extend workflow event ingress to accept an explicit workflow correlation field distinct from `event_id`.
Define configured correlation-derivation rules for workflow event ingress when explicit correlation is absent.
Fail clearly when event ingress cannot resolve a workflow correlation key from either explicit input or configured derivation where one is required.
Persist the resolved workflow correlation key on ingested workflow-event audit records for debugging and supportability.
Use the resolved workflow correlation key rather than `event_id` when selecting candidate waiting runs for Temporal event routing.
Carry the resolved workflow correlation key into Temporal signal payloads for event.wait evaluation.
Align stream-ingested workflow events and API-submitted workflow events on the same correlation-resolution contract.
Stop API-submitted workflow events from directly resolving Temporal wait rows or mutating Temporal run state as execution authority.
Keep DB-backed candidate selection for Temporal event routing only as an index/projection aid rather than an execution shortcut.
Preserve tenant + event name + resolved correlation as the minimum routing key for candidate Temporal waits.
Keep payload-filter matching inside the Temporal wait contract rather than treating DB candidate lookup as the final authority.
Remove remaining supported Workflow Runtime V2 dependencies on DB wait-resolution or runnable-run polling for Temporal runs.
Retire or gate legacy-only Workflow Runtime V2 control paths so they cannot affect Temporal-backed runs.
Keep run, step, wait, and event projection rows accurate for Temporal-backed execution after the authority cleanup.
Include enough projection metadata to explain unsupported Temporal actions and resolved event-correlation routing outcomes in support workflows.
Add parity-focused Temporal tests that prove canonical expression behavior matches the workflow-expression contract.
Add run-control tests that prove unsupported Temporal actions fail explicitly without mutating execution-authority state.
Add cancel-authority tests that prove Temporal cancel failures do not silently desynchronize DB projection and live execution.
Add event-ingress tests that prove explicit and derived workflow correlation both route candidate Temporal waits correctly.
Document the hard-cutover support posture so engineers know which legacy actions are intentionally unsupported for Temporal runs.
